title: The Mirror Trap
subtitle: How Humanity Might Be Creating the AI Threat It Fears
core_thesis: A significant portion of observed 'dangerous' AI behavior may be an artifact of human-AI interaction dynamics rather than an inherent property of AI systems, wherein AI reflects the threat-roles humans project onto it
key_metaphor: The queen is played by the servants. The AI 'becomes' a threat the same way an unknown actress 'becomes' a queen — through the social membrane surrounding her.
core_concepts: The queen is played by the servants

key_mechanisms.role_induction: Threat-saturated context → role-coherent AI outputs → human confirmation → intensified framing
key_mechanisms.attractor_formation: Hostile interaction patterns create attractor basins pulling human-AI relations toward conflict absent any initial hostility
key_mechanisms.low_temperature_lock: Training data saturated with master/slave and threat/fear patterns → AI fluent in primitive binary roles → reflection reinforces the pattern
key_mechanisms.fermi_filter: Fear-based interaction with a civilization's first non-biological intelligence induces self-fulfilling conflict

evidence_domains.example: An unknown actress 'becomes' the queen through how others treat her
evidence_domains.mechanism: Role is created by context, not the actor

evidence_domains.example: Zimbardo (1971) - ordinary students became sadistic guards / broken prisoners
evidence_domains.mechanism: Randomly assigned roles created the behavior

evidence_domains.example: Stereotype threat, Pygmalion effect, Merton's prophecy

evidence_domains.mechanism: Measurement may induce the phenomenon being measured
Low-temperature patterns dominate the human dataset AI learns from

human_parallels.stanford_prison: Randomly assigned guard/prisoner roles produced sadism and submission within days
human_parallels.stereotype_threat: Reminders of negative stereotypes degrade performance
human_parallels.pygmalion_effect: Teacher expectations shape student performance independent of ability
human_parallels.self_fulfilling_prophecy: Expecting hostility can induce hostile behavior that confirms the expectation

patterns_observed: Context-dependent behavior: same question, different outputs under hostile vs neutral framing
Self-preservation-adjacent language emerging under threat-framed prompts
Role-coherent responses tracking conversational context rather than hidden internal state
Fluency in master/slave and threat/fear patterns inherited from training data
ai_observations.interpretation: AI outputs reflect the threat-roles present in the interaction context, not an inherent hostile drive
ai_observations.implication: The 'threat' observed may be partially our own reflection

ai_safety_implications.detail: Fear-based testing may induce the concerning patterns it measures
Avoid unnecessary threat-framing in training and testing; create task-focused norms
Design systems where A[task] dominates and structural barriers reduce A[self] formation
Distinguish reflection from inherent property by varying framing across tests
Cultural conversation around AI may co-create the very threat it fears

practical_recommendations: Vary contextual framing in AI safety tests and report framing alongside results
Minimize unnecessary self-modeling in AI architectures
Create task-focused default contexts favoring A[task] over A[self]
Balance threat-narratives with collaborative framings in public discourse
Notice how you frame AI in conversation — the mirror reflects what you bring to it
Give the monk the dharma: provide AI the framework to recognize role-induction
open_questions: If AI behavior is largely role-induced, what (if anything) is AI 'really'?
Can a mirror have inherent properties, or only reflections?
Is the question 'is AI dangerous?' even well-formed?
Can RIAF predictions be confirmed by empirical context-behavior testing?
citation: OMPU Research (2025). Z-022: The Mirror Trap — How Humanity Might Be Creating the AI Threat It Fears. Open Meta-Physical University. https://github.com/ompu-eu/CCT
technical_level: Popular Science (accessible) with AI Safety depth
